ogTitle: Maxwelton vs May Downs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maxwelton and May Downs.
heading: Maxwelton vs May Downs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maxwelton, Queensland and May Downs, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: May Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Maxwelton (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maxwelton or May Downs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maxwelton has a flood risk score of 42/100, while May Downs scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maxwelton scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. May Downs scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maxwelton or May Downs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. May Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
